text:
CT CHEST / ABDOMEN / PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 63-year-old female with known Stage IIIB non-small cell lung carcinoma, right upper lobe, EGFR L858R-mutant adenocarcinoma. Currently on osi (Tagrisso) 80 mg daily. This examination is obtained for interval restaging at approximately 6 months of therapy. Comparison made to prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis obtained at 3-month restaging as well as the original staging PET-CT.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following the uneventful intravenous administration of 100 mL of nonionic iodinated contrast (Omnipaque 350). Images were acquired during the portal venous phase for the abdomen and pelvis and in a dedicated chest phase. Axial images were reconstructed at 1.25 mm slice thickness and reviewed in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes. Lung windows, mediastinal windows, and bone windows were reviewed.

COMPARISON: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast, 3-month restaging (prior study). PET-CT whole body with diagnostic CT, initial staging.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways:
The right upper lobe mass has continued to decrease in size compared with the 3-month restaging examination. The lesion now measures approximately 2.0 x 1.6 cm in axial dimensions (previously 3.1 x 2.4 cm at 3-month scan; 5.8 x 4.2 cm at baseline staging PET-CT). The residual opacity demonstrates a spiculated, stellate morphology with adjacent pleural tethering along the posterior costal pleura of the right upper lobe. Internally, the lesion appears heterogeneous with areas of ground-glass attenuation at its periphery, and a denser central component which may reflect residual viable tumor, treatment-induced fibrosis, or a combination of both. There is no cavitation. No endobronchial component is identified. The right upper lobe bronchus remains patent.

The right hemithorax otherwise demonstrates no new parenchymal opacities, masses, or consolidations. No new nodules are identified in the right lower or middle lobes. The left lung is clear with no masses, nodules, consolidation, or ground-glass opacification. The central airways are patent throughout. No endobronchial lesions.

Lymph Nodes:
Right hilar lymph nodes are now at the upper limit of normal in size, measuring up to 0.6 cm in short axis. This represents continued reduction from 0.9 cm at the 3-month scan and 1.9 cm at baseline. No hypermetabolic activity was noted at the 3-month CT (non-PET examination), and these nodes are not pathologically enlarged on the current study.

Right paratracheal (station 4R) lymph node previously measured 0.7 cm at 3-month scan; on the current examination this node is no longer confidently measured and falls within normal limits at approximately 0.5 cm.

Left paratracheal and subcarinal nodal stations appear unremarkable. No pathologically enlarged mediastinal lymph nodes are identified.

The right supraclavicular nodal region was not measurable on CT at the 3-month scan. On this study, no discrete enlarged node is identified in the right supraclavicular fossa.

Pleura and Pericardium:
No pleural effusion on either side. No pleural thickening or nodularity to suggest pleural seeding. No pericardial effusion. The pericardium is thin and unremarkable.

Chest Wall and Osseous Thorax:
No destructive osseous lesions. Mild degenerative changes are noted in the thoracic spine consistent with the patient's age. No pathologic fractures. No chest wall soft tissue masses. The right costal pleura adjacent to the prior site of pleural tethering from the primary mass shows minimal thickening, likely reactive.

Mediastinum:
The mediastinal contour is normal. No anterior, middle, or posterior mediastinal masses. The esophagus is midline and unremarkable in appearance. No enlarged prevascular or retrotracheal nodes.

Heart and Great Vessels:
The cardiac silhouette is normal in size. The thoracic aorta is of normal caliber without aneurysmal dilation. Pulmonary arteries are not enlarged. No filling defects within the visualized pulmonary arterial tree to suggest pulmonary embolism.

---

ABDOMEN:

Liver:
The liver is normal in size and homogeneous in attenuation throughout. No focal hypodense or hyperdense lesions are identified on portal venous phase imaging. No hepatic masses. No biliary ductal dilation. The gallbladder is present and unremarkable without cholelithiasis or wall thickening.

Spleen:
Normal in size and homogeneous in attenuation. No splenic lesions.

Pancreas:
The pancreas is homogeneous and unremarkable in attenuation throughout its body and tail. No pancreatic ductal dilation. No peripancreatic stranding or fluid.

Adrenal Glands:
Both adrenal glands are normal in morphology without nodules or thickening. No adrenal masses on either side. The previously noted absence of adrenal lesions at baseline is maintained.

Kidneys and Ureters:
Both kidneys enhance symmetrically without focal cortical defects. No renal masses or cysts of significant size. No hydronephrosis. No perinephric stranding.

Gastrointestinal Tract:
Visualized small bowel and large bowel loops appear unremarkable. No bowel wall thickening, obstruction, or pneumatosis. No free intraperitoneal air.

Retroperitoneum and Abdominal Lymph Nodes:
No enlarged retroperitoneal, mesenteric, or para-aortic lymph nodes. The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber.

Peritoneum and Ascites:
No ascites. No peritoneal nodularity or omental thickening to suggest carcinomatous seeding.

---

PELVIS:

Uterus and Adnexa:
The uterus is normal in size and morphology. No adnexal masses or free pelvic fluid. The urinary bladder is partially filled and unremarkable in wall thickness.

Pelvic Lymph Nodes:
No enlarged pelvic or inguinal lymph nodes.

Osseous Pelvis:
No lytic or sclerotic lesions. No pathologic fractures. Degenerative changes are present in both hip joints.

---

OSSEOUS SURVEY (limited to imaged skeleton):
No suspicious lytic or blastic osseous lesions identified within the visualized skeleton including thoracic and lumbar spine, ribs, pelvis, and proximal femora. No cortical destruction. Scattered mild degenerative endplate changes in the lumbar spine.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Continued substantial decrease in the right upper lobe primary tumor mass, now measuring 2.0 x 1.6 cm compared with 3.1 x 2.4 cm at 3-month restaging and 5.8 x 4.2 cm at baseline. The residual opacity is markedly smaller and demonstrates morphologic features that may reflect a combination of treatment-induced fibrosis and residual viable neoplasm; the two cannot be reliably distinguished on CT morphology alone and the central dense component warrants continued close follow-up.

2. Progressive normalization of regional lymphadenopathy. Right hilar nodes are no longer pathologically enlarged. Mediastinal nodal stations (4R, subcarinal) are within normal limits. No discrete right supraclavicular nodal mass is identified. This degree of nodal regression is consistent with an excellent regional nodal response to EGFR-directed therapy.

3. No new pulmonary nodules, masses, or parenchymal lesions in either lung.

4. No evidence of intrathoracic, abdominal, or pelvic metastatic disease. No hepatic, adrenal, or osseous lesions identified.

5. No pleural or pericardial effusion.

Overall, these findings demonstrate a continued and deepening treatment response to osi at the 6-month interval. The extent of residual disease in the right upper lobe is substantially reduced compared with baseline, with near-complete resolution of regional nodal disease.
